scenario: You are in Professor Kim's sociology class. Your midterm was graded from an old draft after a website upload mistake, and the score is much lower than expected.
direction: Write an email to Professor Kim. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what happened with the submission
Request permission to submit the final draft
Describe how the current grade affects your course standing
subject: Midterm paper submission error

scenario: Your lab partner withdrew from your chemistry project one week before the final presentation, and your section has no clear replacement policy.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Hale. In your email, do the following:
goals: Summarize the group change and timeline
Ask two specific questions about grading and replacement
Propose a workable plan for completing the project
subject: Lab partner withdrawal from group project

scenario: After a guest lecture by a marine biologist, you want to shift your senior thesis topic and need quick guidance before proposal deadlines.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Thompson. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the speaker and reference one concrete idea from the lecture
Connect that idea to your thesis direction
Ask for one or two recommended readings
subject: Thesis direction after your guest lecture

scenario: You missed a quiz because of a same-day clinic visit, and your course policy allows makeups only with timely documentation.
direction: Write an email to your course instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the absence with concise context
Ask what documentation format is required
Request a makeup plan that meets policy
subject: Missed quiz due to medical appointment

scenario: You are a student starting a new semester, and you would like some additional information about an elective course you are considering. You want to make sure it fits your schedule and academic goals.
direction: Write an email to the course instructor, Dr. Adams. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you are interested in the course
Ask specific questions about the course content and requirements
Thank Dr. Adams for taking the time to respond
subject: Request for additional course information

scenario: Your colleague, Daniel, suggested a new café for the team's weekly lunch. You went last Friday, but the wait was over 40 minutes and several orders came out wrong. The team wants to try somewhere else next week.
direction: Write an email to Daniel. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what went wrong at the café
Describe how the team reacted to the experience
Suggest trying a different place next week
subject: Last Friday's team lunch

scenario: You will be attending a three-day industry conference in another city next month. Your colleague, Sarah, attended the same conference last year and has offered to share tips on which sessions are worth attending.
direction: Write an email to Sarah. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain your professional goals for the conference
Ask for her recommendations on sessions and evening activities
Mention any scheduling constraints or personal preferences you have
subject: Advice for the upcoming conference

scenario: You recently attended a career fair at your university and had a brief conversation with a recruiter from a technology company. She encouraged you to apply for their summer internship program and gave you her business card.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Rivera. In your email, do the following:
goals: Remind her of your conversation at the career fair
Express your interest in the summer internship program
Ask about the application process and any deadlines you should be aware of
subject: Follow-up on summer internship opportunity

scenario: You recently started a new part-time job at a local marketing firm. After your first week, you have some questions about the project management software the team uses and the process for submitting work for client review.
direction: Write an email to your team lead, Lisa. In your email, do the following:
goals: Share your positive impressions of the first week
Ask specific questions about the project management tools
Request guidance on the client review submission process
subject: First week questions

scenario: You are a member of your neighborhood community association. You have noticed that the community garden behind the recreation center has become overgrown and some of the raised beds are damaged. The summer planting season is approaching.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Wallace, the association president. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the current condition of the community garden
Explain why it is important to address the issue before summer
Suggest a plan for organizing a volunteer cleanup day
subject: Community garden needs attention

scenario: You participated in a charity 5K run organized by a local nonprofit last weekend. While you enjoyed the event overall, you noticed some issues with the route signage and water station placement that caused confusion for runners.
direction: Write an email to the event coordinator, Ms. Chen. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank her for organizing the event and mention what you enjoyed
Describe the specific issues you noticed during the run
Offer suggestions for improving future events
subject: Feedback on the charity 5K run

scenario: Final exams are three weeks away, and you want to organize a study group with your classmates for your statistics course. You have reserved a study room at the library and have ideas about how to make the sessions productive.
direction: Write an email to your classmates. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you think a study group would be helpful for the final
Describe your plan for the study sessions and topics to cover
Propose a schedule and location for the first meeting
subject: Study group for statistics final

scenario: You attended a weekend workshop on public speaking skills at the community center. The instructor gave practical exercises that helped you feel more confident. You want to continue improving and are wondering if there are follow-up courses.
direction: Write an email to the instructor, Mr. Brooks. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank him for the workshop and explain what you found most helpful
Describe how you plan to apply what you learned
Ask about follow-up courses or additional resources for further practice
subject: Thank you for the public speaking workshop

scenario: You are working on a group project for one of your classes. One of your group members, Jake, has not been contributing and has missed several meetings. The deadline is approaching, and the group needs his part of the work.
direction: Write an email to Jake. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the current status of the project and the upcoming deadline
Explain how his absence has affected the group's progress
Suggest specific ways he can contribute going forward
subject: Need your contribution to the group project

scenario: Your friend Emma has recently moved to a new city and is feeling a bit overwhelmed. She has asked you for advice on how to adjust and make new friends. You have moved several times before and have useful experience.
direction: Write an email to Emma. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how you adjusted when you moved to a new city
Give Emma suggestions on how she can meet new people
Offer to help her in specific ways if she needs more support
subject: Tips for settling into a new city

scenario: Your neighbor, Tom, has been playing loud music late at night for the past week, and it has been difficult for you to sleep. You have an important job interview early next Monday morning and need to rest well this weekend.
direction: Write an email to Tom. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the noise issue and how it has been affecting you
Explain why this weekend is particularly important for you
Suggest a reasonable solution that works for both of you
subject: Noise concern from next door

scenario: You ordered a laptop online two weeks ago for your coursework, but when it arrived, the screen had a visible crack. You contacted customer service by phone but were told to submit a written request.
direction: Write an email to customer service. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the issue with the product you received
Explain how the situation is affecting your ability to complete your work
Request a replacement or refund and ask about the expected timeline
subject: Replacement request for damaged laptop

scenario: You subscribe to a poetry magazine and submitted a poem through its website, but an error appeared after clicking Submit and you received no confirmation email.
direction: Write an email to the editor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention your interest in the magazine and why you decided to submit
Describe the technical issue you experienced during submission
Ask whether your poem was received and what to do if it was not
subject: Poem submission confirmation

scenario: You have recently moved into a new apartment and are experiencing issues with the heating system. The temperature in your unit drops significantly at night, making it uncomfortable to sleep.
direction: Write an email to the property manager, Ms. Green. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the issues you are experiencing with the heating system
Explain how the problem is affecting your daily life
Request a repair and ask for a timeline for when it will be fixed
subject: Heating system repair request
